query: Iran IAEA cooperation law suspension 2025 2026, current state of Iran nuclear inspections and diplomacy, US Iran nuclear deal negotiations status June July 2026
answer:
I've completed thorough research on the Iran-IAEA and US-Iran nuclear situation. Here's the key summary:

## Bottom Line (as of July 13, 2026)

**IAEA Cooperation Law:** Iran's July 2, 2025 law suspending IAEA cooperation **remains in force**. It requires the Supreme National Security Council (SNSC) to authorize any inspection. Iran reaffirmed this framework in June 2026.

**Current Inspection State:** IAEA inspectors have **NOT returned** to Iran's key nuclear sites (per Grossi, July 3, 2026). The agency relies on satellite imagery and cannot verify the ~440.9 kg of 60% enriched uranium last confirmed pre-June-2025. There's an open dispute: Grossi says inspections are coming; Iran says only after a final deal.

**Diplomacy Status — the situation has deteriorated sharply:**
- **June 17, 2026:** The "Islamabad Memorandum" (14-point framework, Pakistan-brokered) ended the 2026 war and opened a 60-day nuclear negotiation window — but it deferred all nuclear/uranium/missile issues.
- **July 8, 2026:** Trump declared the MoU/ceasefire **"over."**
- **July 7–11:** US conducted three strike rounds (>300 targets); Iran retaliated against six nations and **re-closed the Strait of Hormuz.**
- **July 10–12:** Mediators scrambling to de-escalate; the 60-day negotiating window technically runs to mid-August but shows no progress amid active fighting.

A key caveat: this is an extremely fast-moving conflict, and stockpile figures predate the mid-2025 loss of IAEA access, so current quantities are unverifiable. Full structured findings with 16 sourced references have been submitted.

key_facts: Iran's law suspending IAEA cooperation was signed by President Pezeshkian on July 2, 2025 (parliament passed it unanimously June 25, 2025); it requires SNSC authorization for any inspection and remains in force in 2026.
As of July 3, 2026, IAEA Director General Grossi confirmed inspectors had NOT returned to Iran's nuclear facilities and access had been requested but not granted.
The June 2026 Islamabad Memorandum (14-point framework) was signed remotely by Trump and Pezeshkian on June 17, 2026, halting the 2026 Iran war and creating a 60-day window for a final nuclear deal.
The MoU did NOT settle Iran's nuclear program, uranium stockpile, ballistic missiles, or proxies; it only called for down-blending uranium to reactor-grade after a final agreement.

On July 8, 2026, Trump declared the ceasefire/MoU 'over'; US launched three rounds of strikes July 7-11 hitting over 300 targets; Iran retaliated against six nations and declared the Strait of Hormuz closed 'until further notice.'

Iran's position (FM spokesman Baghaei, June 22, 2026) is that IAEA cooperation continues only under existing procedures per parliamentary law and SNSC decisions; Iran says inspections come only after a final deal, contradicting Grossi.

Pre-June-2025 (last verified), Iran held about 440.9 kg of 60% enriched uranium (UF6); total enriched uranium stockpile was reported near 10,000 kg; the IAEA cannot confirm current amounts without access.
Mediators (Qatar, Oman, Pakistan) were scrambling to de-escalate July 10-12, 2026; Trump said talks could continue but voiced pessimism, calling the Iranian regime 'sick people.'
The 2026 war followed Feb 28, 2026 US/Israeli strikes that killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and negotiator Ali Larijani; Mojtaba Khamenei became the new Supreme Leader and endorsed the MoU.
